entities.description: The crop being studied for genetic improvement for drought tolerance.

entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand water-limited conditions.

entities.description: Conventional breeding practices used for genetic improvement.

entities.name: STAY-GREEN
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain green leaf area during drought stress.

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: Approaches using molecular markers to identify genetic factors for trait improvement.

entities.name: STAY-GREEN QTL
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci associated with the stay-green trait.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: External factors that interact with genotype to affect trait expression.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINE
entities.description: A population of homozygous lines derived from crossing two parents.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific growth period during which the sorghum was evaluated.

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: A water regime used to induce drought conditions for evaluation.

entities.name: CONTROL
entities.description: A water regime used as a baseline for comparison against stress.

entities.name: RCBD DESIGN
entities.description: Randomized Complete Block Design used for field experimental layout.

entities.name: PERCENT GREEN LEAF AREA
entities.description: A phenotypic measure used to quantify the stay-green trait.

entities.name: GENETIC LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A map showing the relative positions of markers on a chromosome.

entities.name: SSR
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers used for genetic mapping.

entities.name: DART
entities.description: Diversity Arrays Technology markers used for genetic mapping.

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of DNA to improve crop traits.
